surfaced_from: PWA request 2026-05-25 05:19 UTC — CISSP-like CTO security audit and prioritization
capability_needed: Add deny-by-default host/cloud firewall policy for CTO servers and clone candidates
details: Hetzner API reports no cloud firewalls. Local checks found ufw, nft, and iptables commands unavailable to the cto user. Public listeners include SSH on 0.0.0.0/[::], HTTP/HTTPS for Caddy, and additional clone candidates also have public IPs with no attached firewall. Loopback binding is good for OpenClaw/Hermes internals, but perimeter policy is currently not enforced at the cloud layer.
search_trail: Ran openclaw status and openclaw security audit --deep on cto-v1; result: 0 critical, 1 warn, 1 info; gateway bound to 127.0.0.1 with token auth.
Ran ss -ltnup on cto-v1; found public SSH plus public HTTP/HTTPS and loopback-only OpenClaw/Hermes/A2A services.
Queried Hetzner API for servers, primary IPs, and firewalls; found no cloud firewalls and multiple public CTO servers/candidates.
Inspected /etc/caddy/Caddyfile without secrets; cto.husband.llc reverse-proxies to 127.0.0.1:8088 with security headers and access logging.
Scanned git history/current tree for secret-shaped patterns and key material; confirmed historical VAPID private key material and multiple documented credential-handling risks.
Checked file permissions for /opt/cto, ~/.openclaw, ~/.hermes, ~/.codex; sensitive home config dirs are mostly 0600/0700, while project files are group-writable by cto group.
Checked unattended-upgrades; service is enabled and active.
Attempted privileged sshd/fail2ban verification; elevated execution is unavailable in this PWA runtime, so privileged SSH posture remains unverified.
proposed_resolution: Create a Hetzner firewall for CTO roles: allow 80/443 to PWA, restrict SSH to John/admin source IPs or a tailnet, deny all other inbound, apply to production and candidates by label, and verify lockout-safe access before tightening.
blast_radius: Firewall mistakes can lock out SSH or break cto.husband.llc; applying by label can affect every candidate at once.
rollback_plan: Stage on a candidate first, keep an active SSH session open, use Hetzner console/noVNC as rollback, then remove or loosen the firewall rule set if access breaks.
resolution_notes:
2026-05-27T07:10Z OpenClaw pump: completed a read-only firewall readiness audit. Hetzner inventory still shows 0 cloud firewalls; running servers `recrm` (128886775) and `cto-v1` (130627001) have public IPv4/IPv6 addresses with no cloud firewall attachment. Local cto-v1 listeners are public only for SSH/HTTP/HTTPS while PWA/OpenClaw/Hermes internals remain loopback-only. Wrote a staged deny-by-default candidate policy allowing 80/443 publicly and SSH only from approved John/admin CIDRs or tailnet/bastion, but did not create/apply firewall rules because that could lock out access without an approved SSH source/rollback window. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-007-firewall-readiness-2026-05-27T0710Z.md.

2026-05-27T11:28Z OpenClaw pump: converted the read-only firewall exposure baseline into an approval-ready Hetzner cloud firewall change packet without changing infrastructure. Current evidence still shows 0 Hetzner firewalls, `cto-v1` and `recrm` public with no attached firewall, public listeners limited to SSH/HTTP/HTTPS, and CTO internals loopback-only. Recommended staged policy: allow 80/443 publicly, allow SSH only from John/admin CIDRs or a confirmed tailnet/bastion, deny all other inbound, and attach only after John confirms an SSH source/rollback window. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-007-firewall-approval-packet-2026-05-27T1128Z.md. Status remains open pending approval/window because applying rules could lock out access.
